columns.name: id
columns.description: Human-readable identifier derived from the name (lowercase trimmed)

columns.name: org_id
columns.description: Owning organization for RLS filtering

columns.name: category_name
columns.description: Top-level category name (e.g. Fertilizers, Seeds, Packaging Materials)

columns.name: sub_category_name
columns.description: Subcategory name under the parent category; NULL when this row represents a top-level category

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Soft delete flag; false hides the record from active use

invnt_category.description: Two-level category hierarchy for inventory items in a single table. A row with `sub_category_name IS NULL` is a top-level category (e.g. Fertilizers). A row with `sub_category_name` set is a subcategory under that `category_name` (e.g. Nitrogen Fertilizers under Fertilizers). Both `invnt_category_id` and `invnt_subcategory_id` in `invnt_item` reference this table. - Get all categories: `WHERE sub_category_name IS NULL` - Get subcategories for a category: `WHERE category_name = :name AND sub_category_name IS NOT NULL`

columns.description: Owning organization for RLS filtering

columns.name: farm_id
columns.description: Optional farm scope; NULL if item is shared across farms

columns.name: invnt_category_id
columns.description: Top-level category for item classification; references invnt_category rows where sub_category_name IS NULL

columns.name: invnt_subcategory_id
columns.description: Subcategory for finer item classification; references invnt_category rows where sub_category_name IS NOT NULL

columns.name: invnt_vendor_id

columns.name: onhand_uom

columns.name: pallet_per_truckload

columns.name: cushion_weeks
columns.description: Safety stock buffer in weeks used in next-order-date calculations

columns.name: is_auto_reorder
columns.description: Whether automatic reorder requests are generated when stock hits reorder point

columns.name: reorder_point_in_burn

columns.name: reorder_quantity_in_burn
columns.description: Quantity in burn units to reorder when reorder point is reached

columns.description: Storage site where this item is kept

columns.name: maint_site_id_equipment

columns.description: Whether deliveries and transactions must include a lot number

columns.description: Whether deliveries must include an expiry date

columns.name: grow_variety_id
columns.description: Linked crop variety for seed items

columns.name: seed_is_pelleted
columns.description: Whether seed item is pelleted for easier planting

columns.name: maint_part_type
columns.description: Type classification for parts (e.g. electrical, mechanical, plumbing)

columns.name: maint_part_number

invnt_item.notes:
why do we have storage site and maint storage site?
does it make sense to store variety and pelleted in here? Should there not be a separate seed detail table? Maybe not, but let's discuss. Is lot info just for seeds?
we are storing vendor becasue we define the same product from diff vendors as diff items?
invnt_item.description: The main inventory record. Items belong to an organization and optionally to a specific farm. Classification is handled by the category/subcategory structure. All item details are proper columns grouped by logical sections. Seed-specific fields are prefixed `seed_`; maintenance part fields are prefixed `maint_`.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the purchase order

columns.description: Owning organization for RLS filtering

columns.name: farm_id
columns.description: Optional farm scope for the order

columns.name: request_type
columns.description: Whether this is a non-inventory purchase or an inventory item purchase: non_inventory_item, inventory_item

columns.name: urgency_level
columns.description: How urgently the item is needed: today, 2_days, 7_days, not_urgent

columns.name: invnt_category_id
columns.description: Category for non_inventory_item requests; references invnt_category rows where sub_category_name IS NULL

columns.name: invnt_item_id
columns.description: Linked inventory item; NULL for non_inventory_item requests

columns.name: item_name
columns.description: Snapshot of item name at order time; manually entered for non_inventory_item requests

columns.name: burn_uom
columns.description: Unit of measure for burn quantity (snapshot from item at order time)

columns.name: order_uom
columns.description: Unit of measure for the order quantity (snapshot from item at order time)

columns.description: Quantity ordered in order units

columns.name: is_freight_included
columns.description: Whether total_cost includes freight charges

columns.name: expected_delivery_date

columns.description: Shipping or freight tracking number

columns.name: notes

columns.name: rejected_reason
columns.description: Reason for rejection when status is rejected

columns.description: JSON array of photo URLs attached to the request

columns.name: status
columns.description: Workflow status: requested, approved, rejected, ordered, partial, received, cancelled

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Soft delete flag; false hides the order from active use

columns.name: requested_at
columns.description: Timestamp when the order was requested

columns.name: requested_by
columns.description: Employee who submitted the order request

columns.name: reviewed_at
columns.description: Timestamp when the order was reviewed

columns.name: reviewed_by
columns.description: Employee who approved or rejected the order

columns.name: ordered_at
columns.description: Timestamp when the order was placed with the vendor

columns.name: ordered_by
columns.description: Employee who placed the order with the vendor

invnt_po.description: Tracks purchase order requests through a workflow from request to receipt. Each order snapshots the item name, units, and cost at order time so the record stays accurate even if the item changes later. `request_type` determines whether it's a `non_inventory_item` purchase (classified by `invnt_category_id`) or an `inventory_item` purchase (linked via `invnt_item_id`). `item_name` is always populated either way. Uses `requested_at`/`requested_by` in place of the standard `created_at`/`created_by` audit fields.

invnt_onhand.description: Records on-hand inventory snapshots per item. Each record captures the quantity in onhand units with burn unit conversion and optional lot tracking. Source of truth for computed totals like current stock, burn-per-week, and weeks-on-hand.

invnt_item_summary.description: Dashboard view combining latest on-hand snapshot, open order totals with delivery progress, and computed forecasts. Joins `invnt_item` with the latest `invnt_onhand` record and aggregated open `invnt_po` orders (factoring in `invnt_po_received` deliveries).

invnt_lot_summary.description: Latest on-hand snapshot per unique item and lot number combination. Uses `DISTINCT ON (invnt_item_id, lot_number)` ordered by `onhand_date DESC` to pick the most recent record. Only includes lots with positive stock.

hr_employee.description: Unified employee register and org membership table. Every employee gets a row here with a required `access_level` that defines their role (owner, manager, team_lead, employee). Employees without app access have a null `user_id`. A user can belong to multiple orgs by having one row per org. Tracks employment details, management hierarchy, and compensation.
hr_employee.notes:
what is payroll_id, employee_id?
pay_structure should be an enum or just hourly / salary
I think is_verifier should be in fsafe? It should be assinged by the head of FS not HR, but maybe not worth a dedicated table
access level be enum?

columns.description: Formatted time range for Thursday, or null

columns.name: friday
columns.description: Formatted time range for Friday, or null

columns.name: saturday
columns.description: Formatted time range for Saturday, or null

columns.name: total_hours
columns.description: Total hours worked for the week (sum of completed schedule entries)

columns.name: ot_threshold_weekly
columns.description: Weekly OT threshold derived from `hr_employee.overtime_threshold / 2`; null if not set

columns.name: is_over_ot_threshold
columns.description: True when `total_hours > ot_threshold_weekly`; false if threshold not set

ops_weekly_schedule.description: Pivoted weekly schedule view. One row per employee per task per week. Day columns are formatted as `HH:MM - HH:MM` strings from the schedule start/stop times. Null when the employee did not work that day. Only completed schedule entries (with a `stop_time`) contribute to `total_hours`.

ops_training.description: Staff training session records. Each row is one training event covering a specific topic for a group of employees.

columns.name: atp_site_count
columns.description: Number of sites to randomly select for ATP testing; null means no ATP testing for this template

columns.name: numeric_minimum_rlu_value
columns.description: Minimum acceptable RLU value for ATP tests on this template; results below this are a fail

columns.name: numeric_maximum_rlu_value
columns.description: Maximum acceptable RLU value for ATP tests on this template; results above this are a fail

ops_template.description: Master checklist template. Defines the checklist and the questions employees answer during a task event.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Human-readable identifier derived from name (trimmed lowercase)

columns.name: org_id
columns.description: Owning organization for RLS filtering

columns.name: name
columns.description: Corrective action choice name, unique within the org (e.g. Sanitize Surface, Replace Gloves)

columns.name: description
columns.description: Optional description of what this corrective action entails

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Soft delete flag; false hides the choice from active use

ops_corrective_action_choice.description: Org-defined reusable corrective action options available for selection when logging a corrective action. Users pick from this dropdown; if the action isn't listed they provide a custom description instead.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the question

columns.name: org_id
columns.description: Owning organization for RLS filtering

columns.name: farm_id
columns.description: Optional farm scope; null if the question applies to all farms

columns.name: ops_template_id
columns.description: Checklist template this question belongs to

columns.name: display_order
columns.description: Display order of this question within the template

columns.name: question_text
columns.description: The question or checklist item text shown to the employee

columns.name: response_type
columns.description: Expected response format: boolean, numeric, or enum

columns.name: is_required
columns.description: Whether this question must be answered before the checklist can be submitted

columns.name: boolean_pass_value
columns.description: The boolean value that constitutes a pass; used when response_type is boolean (e.g. true for Yes/Pass, false for No/Pass)

columns.name: numeric_minimum_value
columns.description: Minimum acceptable numeric value; a response below this triggers a corrective action warning

columns.name: numeric_maximum_value
columns.description: Maximum acceptable numeric value; a response above this triggers a corrective action warning

columns.name: enum_options
columns.description: JSON array of all available options for this question; used when response_type is enum (e.g. ["Pass", "Fail", "N/A"])

columns.name: enum_pass_options
columns.description: JSON array of enum options that constitute a pass; responses not in this list trigger a corrective action warning (e.g. ["Pass"])

columns.name: warning_message
columns.description: Custom warning message displayed to the user when the response fails; if null the frontend generates a default message from the pass criteria

columns.name: ops_corrective_action_choice_ids
columns.description: JSON array of ops_corrective_action_choice IDs suggested in the dropdown when this question fails (e.g. ["sanitize_surface", "replace_gloves"]); null shows all active org choices

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Soft delete flag; false hides the question from active checklists

ops_question.description: Questions within a checklist template. Ordered by `display_order` within each template.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the response

columns.name: org_id
columns.description: Owning organization for RLS filtering

columns.name: farm_id
columns.description: Optional farm scope; null if the response applies to all farms

columns.name: ops_task_tracker_id
columns.description: Task tracker session this response belongs to; acts as the checklist completion header

columns.name: ops_template_id
columns.description: Checklist template this response belongs to; every response — checklist or ATP — belongs to a template

columns.name: ops_question_id
columns.description: Checklist question being answered; null for ATP surface test results which have no associated question

columns.name: site_id
columns.description: Site where the ATP test was conducted; null for standard checklist responses which are not site-specific

columns.name: response_boolean
columns.description: Boolean response value; used when question response_type is boolean

columns.name: response_numeric
columns.description: Numeric response value; used when question response_type is numeric

columns.name: response_enum
columns.description: Selected enum option; used when question response_type is enum

columns.name: response_text
columns.description: Free-text notes or observations for this response

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Soft delete flag; false hides the response from active use

ops_response.description: Employee responses to checklist questions. One row per question per task tracker session. The linked `ops_task_tracker` record acts as the header (who completed the checklist, when, and at which site).

columns.name: id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the corrective action

columns.name: org_id
columns.description: Owning organization for RLS filtering

columns.name: farm_id
columns.description: Optional farm scope; null if the corrective action applies to all farms

columns.name: ops_template_id
columns.description: Checklist template this corrective action belongs to; null when triggered by an EMP test result which has no associated template

columns.name: ops_response_id
columns.description: Failing checklist response that triggered this corrective action; null if triggered by an EMP test result

columns.name: fsafe_emp_result_id
columns.description: Failing EMP test result that triggered this corrective action; null if triggered by a checklist response; FK added via ALTER TABLE in fsafe_emp_result migration

columns.name: ops_corrective_action_choice_id
columns.description: Predefined corrective action choice selected from the org lookup; null if a custom description is provided instead

columns.name: other_action
columns.description: Free-text description of the corrective action when no predefined choice is selected

columns.name: assigned_to
columns.description: Employee responsible for completing the corrective action

columns.name: due_date
columns.description: Date by which the corrective action must be completed

columns.name: completed_on
columns.description: Date when the corrective action was completed

columns.name: status
columns.description: Resolution status: open, completed

columns.name: notes
columns.description: Additional notes about the corrective action or its resolution

columns.name: result_description
columns.description: Description of the observed outcome after the corrective action was implemented

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Soft delete flag; false hides the record from active use

columns.name: verified_at
columns.description: Timestamp when the corrective action was verified as effective

columns.name: verified_by
columns.description: Employee who verified the corrective action was effective

ops_corrective_action_taken.description: Corrective actions raised against a failing checklist response or EMP test result. Tracks the action required, who is responsible, and the resolution status.

pack_lot_item.description: Individual products packed within a lot. One row per product per lot.

columns.name: id

columns.description: Harvest quality grade for this product

columns.name: code

columns.name: name
columns.description: Full display name of the product, unique within the farm

columns.name: segment
columns.description: Market segment: wholesale, retail, or food_service

columns.name: description
columns.description: Product description for catalogs and labels

columns.name: pack_packaging_type_id
columns.description: Packaging type for this product; FK to org-defined pack_packaging_type lookup

columns.name: item_uom
columns.description: Unit of measure for the individual product item (e.g. each, head)

columns.name: pack_uom
columns.description: Unit of measure for the consumer pack level (e.g. bag, clamshell)

columns.name: item_per_pack_uom
columns.description: Number of items per pack

columns.name: sale_uom
columns.description: Unit of measure for the sale level (e.g. case, box)

columns.name: pack_per_sale_uom
columns.description: Number of packs per sale unit

columns.name: shipping_uom
columns.description: Unit of measure for the shipping level (e.g. pallet)

columns.name: max_sale_per_shipping_uom
columns.description: Maximum number of sale units the shipping unit can physically hold beyond the standard TI x HI configuration

columns.name: pack_net_weight
columns.description: Net weight of one pack in weight_uom

columns.name: sale_net_weight
columns.description: Net weight of one sale unit in weight_uom

columns.name: shipping_net_weight
columns.description: Net weight of one full shipping unit in weight_uom

columns.name: weight_uom
columns.description: Unit of measure for all net weight values on this product (e.g. lb, kg)

columns.name: sale_uom_length
columns.description: Length of the sale unit in dimension_uom

columns.name: sale_uom_width
columns.description: Width of the sale unit in dimension_uom

columns.name: sale_uom_height
columns.description: Height of the sale unit in dimension_uom

columns.name: dimension_uom
columns.description: Unit of measure for all dimension values on this product (e.g. in, cm)

columns.name: manufacture_storage_method
columns.description: How the product should be stored (e.g. refrigerated, frozen, ambient)

columns.name: minimum_storage_temperature
columns.description: Minimum storage temperature in temperature_uom

columns.name: maximum_storage_temperature
columns.description: Maximum storage temperature in temperature_uom

columns.name: temperature_uom
columns.description: Unit of measure for storage temperature values (e.g. F, C)

columns.name: shelf_life_days
columns.description: Product shelf life in days from date of manufacture

columns.name: shipping_ti
columns.description: TI — number of sale units per layer on the shipping unit

columns.name: shipping_hi
columns.description: HI — number of layers stacked on the shipping unit

columns.name: shipping_requirements
columns.description: Special shipping instructions (e.g. temperature range, handling notes)

columns.name: is_catch_weight
columns.description: Whether this product is sold by actual weight rather than fixed weight

columns.name: is_hazardous
columns.description: Whether this product is classified as hazardous material

columns.name: is_fsma_traceable
columns.description: Whether this product is on the FDA FSMA 204 Food Traceability List

columns.name: gtin
columns.description: Global Trade Item Number (up to 14 digits)

columns.name: upc
columns.description: Universal Product Code (up to 12 digits)

columns.name: photos
columns.description: JSON array of photo URLs for the product

columns.name: display_order
columns.description: Sort order for UI display within the farm

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Soft delete flag; false hides the product from active use

sales_product.description: The sellable products from each farm. Combines a grade with a full packaging hierarchy (item → pack → sale → shipping) that drives inventory calculations. Columns are grouped by logical sections: identity, packaging quantities, weights, dimensions, storage, shipping, flags, and identification. All net weight values share `weight_uom`, all dimensions share `dimension_uom`, and all temperatures share `temperature_uom`.

maint_request.description: Standalone maintenance work order requests. Tracks site issues, preventive maintenance, urgency, scheduling, fixer assignment, completion details, and before/after photos. Uses `requested_at`/`requested_by` in place of the standard `created_at`/`created_by` audit fields.

maint_request_invnt_item.description: Inventory items consumed during a maintenance request. One row per item per request.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Human-readable unique identifier derived from org and test name

columns.name: org_id
columns.description: Owning organization for RLS filtering

columns.name: test_name
columns.description: Name of the test or pathogen being tested for (e.g. Listeria, Salmonella)

columns.name: test_methods
columns.description: JSON array of available test methods users can select when recording a result (e.g. ["PCR", "Culture", "ELISA"])

columns.name: test_description
columns.description: Optional description of the test and its purpose

columns.name: enum_options
columns.description: JSON array of all selectable result options when result_type is enum (e.g. ["Detected", "Not Detected"])

columns.name: enum_pass_options
columns.description: JSON array of enum values that constitute a passing result (e.g. ["Not Detected"])

columns.name: numeric_minimum_value
columns.description: Minimum acceptable numeric value; results below this are a fail

columns.name: numeric_maximum_value
columns.description: Maximum acceptable numeric value; results above this are a fail

columns.name: required_retests
columns.description: Number of retest records to auto-generate when any test of this type fails

columns.name: required_vector_tests
columns.description: Number of vector test records to auto-generate when any test of this type fails

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Soft delete flag; false hides the record from active use

fsafe_emp_test.description: Catalog of EMP test definitions and their result configuration. Defines how results are evaluated and how many retests or vector tests are required on a fail.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the test result record

columns.name: org_id
columns.description: Owning organization for RLS filtering

columns.name: farm_id
columns.description: Farm where the sample was collected

columns.name: fsafe_emp_test_id
columns.description: EMP test definition used for this test event

columns.name: site_id
columns.description: Site where the sample was collected; zone classification is stored on the site record

columns.name: test_method
columns.description: Test method used, selected from the test methods list on the EMP test definition (e.g. PCR, Culture)

columns.name: initial_retest_vector
columns.description: Type of test: initial (first run), retest (triggered by any fail), vector (triggered by any fail)

columns.name: status
columns.description: Workflow status: pending, in_progress, completed

columns.name: result_enum
columns.description: Enum result value selected from test enum_options when result_type is enum

columns.name: result_numeric
columns.description: Numeric result value when result_type is numeric; frontend converts detection limit strings (e.g. <1, >2419) to numeric values before submission

columns.name: results_pass
columns.description: Whether the result meets the pass criteria defined on the EMP test definition

columns.name: warning_message
columns.description: Warning message displayed when the result fails

columns.name: fail_code
columns.description: Human-readable failure code assigned to this test result (e.g. LM-001)

columns.name: original_fsafe_emp_result_id
columns.description: Reference to the initial test result that triggered this retest or vector test; null for initial tests

columns.name: notes
columns.description: Free-text notes about the test event

columns.name: sampled_at
columns.description: Timestamp when the sample was collected

columns.name: sampled_by
columns.description: Employee who collected the sample

columns.name: completed_at
columns.description: Timestamp when the lab completed processing the sample

columns.name: verified_at
columns.description: Timestamp when the test result was verified

columns.name: verified_by
columns.description: Employee who verified the test result

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Soft delete flag; false hides the record from active use

fsafe_emp_result.description: EMP test results. One row per test event. Retests and vector tests link back to the original failing test via `original_fsafe_emp_result_id`, forming a clear chain of why each test was created. Detection limit values (e.g. `<1`, `>2419`) are converted to numeric values by the frontend before submission.

isn't test method defined in the test?

When a product is packed or sold, this mapping determines which inventory items are consumed and in what quantity. Migration: `supabase/migrations_future/20260316_001_invnt_sales_product_item.sql`

invnt_usage.description: Tracks inventory consumption linked back to the source module that triggered it. The `reference_table` and `reference_id` columns provide a generic FK to any table (e.g. grow_fertigation_schedule, harvest_batch) so usage can be traced to its origin. Migration: `supabase/migrations_future/20260316_002_invnt_usage.sql`

hr_travel_request.description: Employee travel requests with a simple approval workflow. Captures trip details, purpose, and dates alongside a pending → approved/denied status flow. Uses `requested_at` in place of the standard `created_at`/`created_by` audit fields. Migration: `supabase/migrations_future/20260316_003_hr_travel_request.sql`

hr_disciplinary_warning.description: Employee disciplinary warning records. Tracks the offense, action plan, and employee acknowledgment alongside a pending → reviewed workflow. Migration: `supabase/migrations_future/20260316_004_hr_disciplinary_warning.sql`

org_farm.description: Represents a crop or product line within an organization (e.g. Cuke Farm, Lettuce Farm). Each farm has its own sites, varieties, grades, and products. Farm-level defaults reference units of measure for weighing and growing operations.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Human-readable identifier derived from site name (trimmed lowercase)

columns.name: org_id
columns.description: Owning organization for RLS filtering

columns.name: farm_id
columns.description: Optional farm scope; NULL if site is shared across the org

columns.name: name
columns.description: Display name of the site, unique within the org+farm combination

columns.name: category
columns.description: Top-level classification selected from dropdown (e.g. growing, packaging, storage, maintenance)

columns.name: subcategory
columns.description: Second-level classification within category (e.g. greenhouse, nursery, packroom, equipment, vehicle)

columns.name: acres
columns.description: Acreage of the growing site

columns.name: total_rows
columns.description: Total number of growing rows in the site

columns.name: avg_units_per_row
columns.description: Average number of growing units (plants/pots) per row

columns.name: code
columns.description: Short identifier for equipment/assets (e.g. PUMP-01, TRACTOR-03); nullable for non-equipment sites

columns.name: manufacturer
columns.description: Manufacturer or brand name for equipment/assets

columns.name: model
columns.description: Model name or number for equipment/assets

columns.name: serial_number
columns.description: Manufacturer serial number for equipment/assets

columns.name: purchase_date
columns.description: Date the equipment/asset was acquired

columns.name: manual_url
columns.description: URL or path to the equipment manual or site documentation

columns.name: notes
columns.description: General notes about the site or asset

columns.name: photos
columns.description: JSON array of photo URLs

columns.name: metadata
columns.description: Flexible JSON for display-only details (dimensions, capacity, environmental settings)

columns.name: is_food_contact_surface
columns.description: Whether this site or surface comes into contact with food; requires sanitization before reuse if true

columns.name: zone
columns.description: EMP zone classification for this site as defined in food safety documentation: zone_1, zone_2, zone_3, zone_4; null if not applicable

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Soft delete flag; false hides the site from active use

org_site.description: Unified site register for all physical locations and assets across the organization. The `category` and `subcategory` fields drive which additional fields are relevant in the UI. Sites can be scoped to a specific farm or shared org-wide. Categories and subcategories: growing (greenhouse, nursery), packaging (packroom, cold_storage), storage (warehouse, chemical_storage), maintenance (equipment, vehicle, infrastructure).

comments: I beleive now your .md includes a generic command to build four CRUD columns. Should we add is_active to this command

It's kind of interesting, and maybe not true, but are the three foundational tables: people who work at the farm, the physical areas of the farm, and inventory items that come into the farm. I wonder what doesn't at some point touch one of these tables?
will we have a table that defines what tables / forms a user can edit - for example, maybe head of HR or owners can manager hr_department or hr_titles definitions
I haven't reviewed fully, but are all nullable characteristsic correct?
